sentence_reviews.text: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning adapts a frozen or mostly frozen model through small trainable modules; LoRA learns low-rank updates to selected weight matrices.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA arXiv abstract lines 42-44 says LoRA freezes pretrained model weights and injects trainable rank-decomposition matrices into Transformer layers, greatly reducing trainable parameters for downstream tasks.
sentence_reviews.notes: The LoRA-specific part is directly supported; small trainable modules is a fair generalization from injected trainable matrices.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning, Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Parameter-efficient tuning, adapters & LoRA is modeled as depending on Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning, Linear algebra and feeding downstream nodes such as Large language models, Code models, copilots & repository intelligence, Model merging & weight-space composition.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
